content: When a ticket's acceptance check greps (`grep -F`) for a literal contiguous phrase that spans a token also used as an inline-code span elsewhere (e.g. a slash command), ensure at least one BARE plain-text occurrence of the full phrase exists outside any backticks. A backtick adjacent to the phrase boundary (e.g. `` `...improve` `` ` to automate`) silently breaks the substring match. Verify by inspecting the byte immediately before/after the phrase, not just that the words appear.
context: T-020 (je-dev-skills): SKILL.md §5 had to satisfy `grep -F "prompt-engineering-improve to automate"`. The user-facing pointer wanted the slash-command form `/je-dev-skills:prompt-engineering-improve` (backticked), which would have put a backtick before the space and failed the grep. Fix: a separate bare occurrence "invoke prompt-engineering-improve to automate the loop". Caught pre-implementation by extracting the draft to a temp file and checking the byte before the phrase was a plain space (index 1119).
title: grep -F verification tokens need a bare plain-text occurrence outside code spans
